name: Oronde Gadsden
headline: Breakout rookie TE locks in weekly starter status
body:
Gadsden's rookie year was an outright breakout for a fifth-round pick. Over 15 games on a 62.6 percent snap share, the 22-year-old ran 381 routes and converted a 12.7 percent target share into 49 catches for 664 yards (13.55 per reception) and three touchdowns, totaling 82.4 fantasy points and a TE11 finish. The efficiency is excellent: 1.74 yards per route run, a 0.286 WOPR, and a 0.239 EPA per play with a 63.8 percent success rate. He was a true downfield seam threat, with 11 grabs of 20-plus yards and a 53-yard long.

The red-zone usage cements the ceiling: 15 red-zone targets and five end-zone looks, room to grow the three-score output. Six drops on 55 catchable balls is the lone blemish. With an entrenched role in the Chargers' offense and rising target volume, he profiles as a low-end TE1 with upside for more. He is a weekly starter in all formats and a strong buy in dynasty given his age and trajectory.
